etymology.description: 爪(爪) (claw; hand) + 卩(卩) (kneeling person) -> complexion
etymology.interpretation: The combination suggests a person being grasped or manipulated, leading to an association with 'complexion' as a surface appearance. This concept of outward appearance then extends metaphorically to ‘color’. As complexion can also imply an aspect of allure or aesthetic appeal, it further extends to the sense of ‘sex’, where visual appeal plays a central role.

examples.chinese: 这个季节的颜色非常丰富(這個季節的顏色非常豐富)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège jìjié de yánsè fēicháng fēngfù.
examples.english: The colors of this season are very rich.

examples.chinese: 他的画用色大胆(他的畫用色大膽)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de huà yòng sè dàdǎn.

examples.chinese: 电影中对色彩的运用非常独特(電影中對色彩的運用非常獨特)。
examples.pinyin: Diànyǐng zhōng duì sècǎi de yùnyòng fēicháng dútè.
examples.english: The use of color in the film is very unique.
